matchup: San Antonio Spurs @ Oklahoma City Thunder
series_context: Western Conference Finals Game 5; SAS elimination prep required for Game 6
source_files_consulted: User-provided player tracking rebounding and box-out tables

final_score.note: User closing-line screen lists SAS 114. NBA official scorer PDF page 1 lists SAS 113. Treat as one-point reconciliation flag until official feed finalizes. Market grading for SAS TT over/under around 106.5-108.5 is unaffected.

full_game.spread_cluster: OKC -3.5 to -4.0; best visible OKC -3.5 -109, SAS +3.5 +105

rebound_environment.classification: OKC controlled defensive rebound environment with Hartenstein anchoring contested-board capture; SAS generated volume offensive boards but converted less efficiently than OKC.

Isaiah Hartenstein.signal: elite board control and box-out conversion

Chet Holmgren.signal: high opportunity share with low contested load; stable complement to Hartenstein

Victor Wembanyama.signal: high chances but poor capture rate; under profile strengthened

Cason Wallace.signal: guard long-board profile but low raw ceiling

scoring_environment.key_read: OKC beat expected scoring through FT volume plus elite three-point shot-making, not superior shot quality. SAS shot quality was slightly higher, but OKC produced a massive efficiency edge.
scoring_environment.shot_distribution_note: SAS had 76.09% rim-or-3 frequency and 0.56 shot quality but only 46.74% eFG due to 12/41 from three. OKC had lower rim-or-3 frequency at 61.45% and lower shot quality at 0.54 but hit 14/32 from three.

turnover_environment.signal: OKC had higher turnover danger but compensated with shooting/FTs/rebounding. SAS could not fully monetize OKC live-ball leakage. For Game 6, OKC live-ball turnover risk remains a comeback path for SAS but not enough alone without SAS three-point correction.

okc.injury_context: Jalen Williams inactive; OKC absorbed usage through Shai, McCain, Caruso and double-big boards.
okc.high_leverage_lineup: Caruso-Hartenstein-SGA-Wallace-McCain logged +14 in 8:04 with 27 points on 17 possessions and zero turnovers.
okc.risk: Hartenstein turnover pocket appeared in some lineups but rebound/control value outweighed it.

sas.core_issue: Wembanyama logged 37.6 minutes but only 6 rebounds on 16 chances and no box-outs; SAS wing/guard board capture absorbed his missed rebound volume.
sas.late_game_distortion: Bench scoring after 2:13 and extended closing possessions added team-total over fuel and invalidated earlier live under read.

okc_side_edge.assumption: OKC interior/control edge should translate to spread cover.
okc_side_edge.actual: OKC won by 13/14 depending feed; cover confirmed.

okc_team_total_over.assumption: OKC scoring ceiling alive through SGA FT pressure and secondary scoring.

sas_team_total_under_liveflow.assumption: Lead suppression and fourth-quarter pace would cap SAS below 111.5.
sas_team_total_under_liveflow.actual: SAS reached 113/114 after late extension and bench scoring.

wemby_rebound_caution.assumption: Avoid aggressive Wemby rebound overs vs OKC double-big/box-out environment.
wemby_rebound_caution.actual: 6 rebounds on 16 chances with 37.5% chance conversion.

primary_questions: Does SAS reduce Wembanyama perimeter volume and improve rebound positioning?
Does OKC keep Hartenstein above 28 minutes after 15-rebound control game?
Does SAS three-point volume remain high despite 29.27% outcome?
Does OKC turnover leakage regress or invite SAS upset/cover pressure?
Does market overreact to OKC offensive efficiency even though OKC shot quality was lower than SAS?
early_game6_angles_to_validate: Hartenstein rebounds over / OREB ladder if line opens conservative
Wembanyama rebounds under if line remains reputation-inflated
OKC team total caution if 3PT efficiency inflated market above sustainable shot quality
SAS team total over only if underpriced below 108 and pace stays around 101 possessions
LiveFlow avoid SAS TT unders unless lead >18 with under 5:00 or bench scoring risk fully removed

rolling_window_status.confidence: HIGH for rebound patches; MEDIUM for scoring carryover due to OKC 3PT variance; LOW for SAS live team-total unders without garbage-time filter
